senses.definition: a sign placed along roads or highways to convey information, instructions, or warnings for regulating traffic and ensuring road safety

examples.x: He studied the meaning of each traffic sign before taking his driving test.
Some traffic signs are hard to see at night without proper lighting.
The no-parking traffic sign was ignored by several drivers.
She slowed down when she saw the speed limit traffic sign.
He pulled over to check a traffic sign that seemed unclear.

senses.definition: a sign that shows warnings or information to drivers

examples.x: The road sign warned drivers of a sharp curve ahead.
He slowed down after seeing the road sign for a pedestrian crossing.
The road sign indicated a speed limit of 50 kilometers per hour.
She missed her turn because the road sign was obscured by a tree.
The road sign showed the distance to the next gas station.

senses.definition: a digital display board that shows different information depending on the situation or need

examples.x: Variable-message signs on highways provide real-time traffic updates to help drivers plan their routes.
In cities, variable-message signs inform pedestrians about upcoming events or changes in public transport schedules.
During emergencies, variable-message signs display important instructions and evacuation routes to ensure public safety.
At airports, variable-message signs guide passengers to their gates and inform them about flight delays or cancellations.
Variable-message signs at construction sites warn drivers about lane closures and provide alternate routes to avoid congestion.

senses.definition: a type of traffic sign that provides specific instructions or regulations to road users, typically in the form of symbols or words

examples.x: When you see a regulatory sign with a speed limit displayed, you must adhere to that speed to ensure safety on the road.
At intersections, regulatory signs often dictate whether you can turn left, right, or proceed straight ahead.
The regulatory sign at the school zone indicates when drivers must slow down because children may be crossing the street.
A no parking sign is a common example of a regulatory sign used to enforce parking restrictions in certain areas.
Drivers must obey the regulatory sign indicating a one-way street, ensuring traffic flows in the designated direction.

senses.definition: a red, octagonal traffic sign that indicates vehicles must come to a complete stop

examples.x: When you see a stop sign at an intersection, you must stop your car before proceeding.
The stop sign on Main Street is faded and needs to be repainted for better visibility.
Drivers who ignore a stop sign risk causing accidents and receiving traffic citations.
It's important to teach new drivers about the meaning and importance of obeying stop signs.
The municipality installed additional stop signs along the residential street to improve safety for pedestrians.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that indicates drivers must give the right of way to vehicles from another direction

examples.x: When you approach a yield sign, you should slow down and be prepared to stop if necessary.
The yield sign at the intersection reminded drivers to be cautious and yield to oncoming traffic.
It is important to obey the yield sign to avoid accidents and maintain traffic flow.
Drivers must yield at a yield sign even if they have a green light, if turning would conflict with oncoming traffic.
Look carefully for pedestrians and cyclists near a yield sign, as they also have the right of way in some situations.

senses.definition: a traffic symbol indicating that vehicles are not permitted to turn to the right at a specified location

examples.x: At the intersection, there was a no right turn sign, so I had to continue straight ahead.
The driver ignored the no right turn sign and made an illegal maneuver.
We were confused by the no right turn sign and had to find an alternative route.
The city installed a new no right turn sign to improve pedestrian safety.
Remember to always obey the no right turn sign to avoid traffic violations.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that prohibits vehicles from turning left at a specific intersection or location

examples.x: At the busy junction, the no left turn sign prevented drivers from making a leftward maneuver during rush hour.
The driver glanced at the no left turn sign and obediently continued straight ahead.
Following the no left turn sign's instructions, the bus driver patiently waited for the traffic light to change.
Ignoring the no left turn sign, the cyclist veered left and was promptly stopped by a traffic officer.
A clear understanding of the no left turn sign is crucial for navigating unfamiliar city streets safely.

senses.definition: a traffic sign indicating that vehicles are not permitted to make turns at a specific location

examples.x: Drivers must obey the no turns sign at this intersection to ensure smooth traffic flow.
The no turns sign was recently installed to improve pedestrian safety in the area.
If you see a no turns sign, you should find an alternative route to reach your destination.
The no turns sign on this street corner prevents vehicles from entering the residential area.
Ignoring the no turns sign can result in a fine or penalty from law enforcement officers.

senses.definition: a traffic signal indicating that vehicles are prohibited from reversing direction at that point
a notice indicating where parking is prohibited

examples.x: The no parking sign on the street corner forbids vehicles from stopping there.
Drivers should always obey the no parking sign to avoid fines or towing.
A clear no parking sign helps maintain traffic flow and pedestrian safety.
Ignoring a no parking sign can inconvenience others and lead to congestion.
It's important to look for a no parking sign before leaving your car in any unfamiliar area.

senses.definition: a red and white sign indicating that entry is prohibited

examples.x: He immediately halted at the do not enter sign near the intersection.
Please adhere to the do not enter sign to avoid traffic violations.
The do not enter sign was prominently displayed on the gate to the private property.
She paused before crossing the do not enter sign on the hiking trail.
A new do not enter sign was installed at the entrance to the construction zone.

senses.definition: a sign that indicates pedestrians are not allowed to cross the road at that point

examples.x: The intersection had a prominent no pedestrians crossing sign, directing walkers to use nearby crosswalks.
Drivers must observe areas marked with a no pedestrians crossing sign to prevent accidents involving pedestrians crossing illegally.
In urban areas, you'll often find no pedestrians crossing signs at intersections to ensure smooth traffic flow.
Please use designated crosswalks; ahead is an area marked with a no pedestrians crossing sign.
The no pedestrians crossing sign at the busy intersection reminded pedestrians to prioritize using designated crosswalks for their safety.

senses.definition: a sign that shows the maximum speed allowed on a road

examples.x: The speed limit sign on the highway says 60 kilometers per hour.
Many drivers look at the speed limit sign to know how fast they can go.
The speed limit sign near the school is 30 kilometers per hour.
It is important to obey the speed limit sign to keep everyone safe.
A new speed limit sign was put up because of the construction work on the road.

senses.definition: a traffic signal indicating that vehicles must turn left at an intersection

examples.x: The driver approached the intersection and noticed the left turn only sign, signaling that they could only proceed in the direction indicated.
Following the left turn only sign, all vehicles in the left lane turned left onto the narrow street.
It is important for drivers to obey the left turn only sign to avoid accidents and ensure smooth traffic flow.
The city council installed a left turn only sign at the busy intersection to regulate the movement of vehicles during peak hours.
Drivers unfamiliar with the area were confused by the left turn only sign, causing delays in traffic as they waited to turn.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that instructs drivers to turn only to their right at an intersection

examples.x: At the crossroads, the right turn only sign directed all vehicles to make a turn to the right.
She carefully approached the intersection, checking for the right turn only sign before changing lanes.
The driver missed the right turn only sign and had to circle back to the intersection.
Following the right turn only sign, he smoothly merged into the lane designated for turning.
The right turn only sign ensured a smooth flow of traffic during rush hour.

senses.definition: a sign that shows drivers they can only go straight and not turn

examples.x: The driver saw the straight through only sign and kept going forward.
At the intersection, there was a straight through only sign, so cars could not turn left or right.
The straight through only sign helps keep traffic moving in one direction.
Drivers must follow the straight through only sign to avoid getting a ticket.
The new straight through only sign made it easier to cross the busy road.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that tells drivers to go around a circular intersection in one direction

examples.x: The roundabout circulation sign shows that cars must turn to the right.
Drivers see the roundabout circulation sign before they enter the circle.
The roundabout circulation sign helps to keep the traffic moving smoothly.
You must follow the roundabout circulation sign to avoid accidents.
Look for the roundabout circulation sign when you approach the intersection.

senses.definition: a sign shaped like an X that marks a railroad crossing

examples.x: The train stopped when the crossbuck sign showed up.
Drivers must look for the crossbuck to know a train is coming.
The crossbuck sign is very important for road safety.
Children learned about the crossbuck sign in their safety class.
A crossbuck sign was placed near the new railroad track.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that tells drivers about possible danger or changes in the road ahead

examples.x: The yellow triangle is a warning sign for a sharp turn ahead.
The warning sign shows there is a school nearby, so drivers should slow down.
A deer crossing warning sign means animals might be on the road.
The warning sign with a falling rocks symbol tells drivers to be careful.
Roadwork ahead is marked by a warning sign with an orange background.

senses.definition: a road sign that shows where two or more roads meet

examples.x: The driver saw the crossroad sign and slowed down.
At the crossroad sign, you need to decide which way to go.
The crossroad sign had arrows pointing in different directions.
Be careful when you see a crossroad sign because other cars may be coming.
The map showed a crossroad sign where the streets intersected.

senses.definition: a road sign that shows cars go in both directions on the same road

examples.x: The two-way traffic sign means cars can come from both sides.
Be careful when you see a two-way traffic sign.
The two-way traffic sign was clear on the narrow road.
The new driver learned about the two-way traffic sign in class.
The driver slowed down at the two-way traffic sign.

senses.definition: a sign that shows people where they can safely cross the road

examples.x: The pedestrian crossing sign helps children cross the street safely near the school.
Drivers must stop when they see the pedestrian crossing sign.
The new pedestrian crossing sign was put up near the park.
At night, the pedestrian crossing sign is very bright and easy to see.
People feel safer when there is a pedestrian crossing sign at busy roads.

senses.definition: a road sign that shows animals might cross the road there

examples.x: The driver slowed down when he saw the wildlife crossing sign.
A wildlife crossing sign means animals like deer may be on the road.
It is important to watch for animals when you see a wildlife crossing sign.
The wildlife crossing sign helps protect both animals and drivers.
There was a wildlife crossing sign near the forest on the highway.

senses.definition: a warning sign that tells people the ground might be slippery when it is wet

examples.x: The slippery when wet sign is next to the swimming pool.
Be careful when you see the slippery when wet sign.
The janitor put up a slippery when wet sign after mopping the floor.
The slippery when wet sign helps to prevent accidents.
Drivers slow down when they see a slippery when wet sign on the road.

senses.definition: a traffic indication that alerts drivers to ongoing construction or maintenance work ahead on the road
a traffic sign that indicates the presence of a circular intersection where traffic flows in a counter-clockwise direction

examples.x: When you see a roundabout sign ahead, remember to yield to traffic already in the circle.
The roundabout sign helps drivers navigate intersections more smoothly by encouraging a continuous flow of traffic.
Look for the roundabout sign at the entrance to the junction to know when to slow down and yield.
Drivers should always check for pedestrians and cyclists near a roundabout sign before entering the intersection.
In some countries, a roundabout sign is also known as a traffic circle sign, indicating the same type of intersection design.

senses.definition: a traffic signal indicating that a person directing traffic is up ahead

examples.x: The flagger ahead sign is usually yellow with a symbol of a person holding a flag, helping motorists anticipate the need to slow down and be cautious.
Upon seeing a flagger ahead sign, drivers should reduce their speed and be ready to follow the directions given by the flagger.
The flagger ahead sign is placed strategically before construction zones or areas where roadwork is being conducted to ensure safety for both drivers and workers.
Paying attention to the flagger ahead sign is crucial for avoiding accidents and ensuring smooth traffic flow during road maintenance activities.
When driving on highways, it's important to watch out for a flagger ahead sign, as it warns drivers to prepare for traffic control.

senses.definition: a type of sign that provides directions or information to travelers

examples.x: When driving on unfamiliar roads, guide signs help drivers find their way to different destinations.
At the airport, large guide signs indicate where to check in for flights or find baggage claim areas.
In a city, guide signs on street corners show pedestrians which way to walk to reach popular landmarks.
Tourists rely on guide signs in museums to navigate through exhibits and find specific artworks or artifacts.
On hiking trails, guide signs are placed at intersections to ensure hikers stay on the correct path to their destination.

senses.definition: a marker placed along roads and highways to indicate the location where vehicles can leave or exit from the main road or freeway

examples.x: Drivers should follow the exit signs carefully to reach their intended destinations.
The exit sign approaching the highway interchange was clearly marked with directions.
Missing an exit sign can lead to delays in reaching your destination.
Exit signs are typically placed well in advance to allow drivers to change lanes safely.
During heavy traffic, it's important to pay attention to exit signs to avoid last-minute lane changes.

senses.definition: a notice or symbol indicating the presence or location of a hospital

examples.x: When driving through the city, I saw a hospital sign pointing towards the nearest emergency room.
The hospital sign outside the building was bright red and easy to spot from the street.
Please follow the hospital signs to find the maternity ward; it's on the third floor.
She looked for the hospital sign to guide her to the correct entrance for outpatient services.
They placed a new hospital sign at the corner of the road to improve visibility for incoming patients.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that indicates the location or direction to a fueling station
a traffic sign that indicates the location or direction to a bus station

examples.x: At the corner of the street, there is a bus station sign showing the way to the nearest terminal.
Look for the bus station sign if you're unsure where to catch the bus.
The bus station sign helped me find my way to the bus stop in the unfamiliar city.
Make sure to follow the bus station sign to reach the correct platform.
The bus station sign was clear and easy to understand, guiding us through the busy city streets.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that provides directions and information for railway passengers
a traffic sign is a road sign displaying symbols or logos to indicate nearby services or facilities for drivers

examples.x: On the highway, logo signs direct drivers to nearby gas stations and restaurants.
The logo sign ahead indicated there was a rest area with facilities.
She noticed the logo sign for a motel, prompting her to take the upcoming exit.
Logo signs are strategically placed to help motorists find services quickly.
Drivers rely on logo signs to locate amenities like food and lodging during their journeys.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that indicates an area near a school where drivers should exercise caution and adhere to reduced speed limits

examples.x: When you see a school zone sign, remember to slow down because children might be crossing the street.
The school zone sign is yellow with black symbols to alert drivers of the nearby school.
Drivers must always obey the school zone sign, especially during morning and afternoon hours when students are arriving or leaving.
It is important for drivers to be aware of the school zone sign to ensure the safety of pedestrians, including students and crossing guards.
The school zone sign helps create a safer environment by reminding drivers to watch for children and follow the designated speed limit.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that displays the name of a particular road or street

examples.x: When driving in a new area, look out for street name signs to find your way around easily.
The city council installed new street name signs last month to improve navigation for residents and visitors alike.
It's important to obey street name signs to avoid getting lost in unfamiliar neighborhoods.

senses.definition: an electronic traffic sign that displays real-time information about road conditions or alerts to drivers

examples.x: When approaching a busy highway, dynamic message signs provide updates on traffic congestion and suggested alternate routes.
During severe weather, dynamic message signs instruct drivers to reduce speed and proceed with caution.
In urban areas, dynamic message signs inform motorists of upcoming road closures due to construction.
At night, dynamic message signs use bright LED lights to enhance visibility and communicate important safety messages.
Drivers should always pay attention to dynamic message signs for updates on accidents or hazards ahead.

senses.definition: a maker along a road that indicates the distance from a specific starting point or reference point, typically in miles

examples.x: The milepost indicated that the next rest area was 10 miles ahead.
He noted the milepost numbers to track his progress on the highway.
They installed new mileposts along the highway for better navigation.
The milepost was placed every mile along the interstate highway.
She checked the milepost to estimate the remaining distance to her destination.

senses.definition: a marker or sign along a road that indicates a specific point or achievement, such as a significant distance traveled or a historical event

examples.x: The milestone marked the halfway point of their cross-country journey.
She paused at the milestone to take a photo of the scenic view.
He reached a personal milestone of driving 100,000 miles in his car.
They celebrated the milestone of completing construction on the new highway.
The milestone commemorated the founding of the town 200 years ago.

senses.definition: a sign that provides information such as the distance to a certain place or its direction, usually found at the side of a road
a destination displayed on traffic signs to guide drivers towards their desired routes

examples.x: The highway sign indicated that Toronto was the control city for the westbound lanes.
Before entering the highway, drivers checked the signs to find the control city for their journey.
On the interstate, New York was prominently listed as the control city for all southbound traffic.
Following the directions to the control city, motorists exited the freeway at the designated interchange.
It's essential for drivers to understand which city is the control city to navigate efficiently on unfamiliar roads.

senses.definition: a traffic sign that provides confirmation and direction along a route
a traffic sign that indicates the route number and direction on major roads
a painted line or symbol on a road that guides drivers and helps organize traffic flow
a type of traffic sign that can be easily removed or relocated as needed

examples.x: Demountable copies of traffic signs are often used in temporary construction zones to guide drivers safely around road work.
In urban areas, demountable copies help regulate parking during events by indicating temporary no-parking zones.
Highway maintenance crews rely on demountable copies to alert drivers of upcoming road closures or detours ahead.
Demountable copies must meet strict visibility and durability standards to ensure they remain effective and visible to drivers in various weather conditions.
During emergencies, demountable copies play a crucial role in directing traffic away from hazardous areas or toward designated evacuation routes.

senses.definition: the text or wording displayed on a traffic sign
a reflective road sign used to warn other drivers of a stopped vehicle ahead due to a breakdown or accident
